intro: Origen of Alexandria (c. 185–253) was the most prolific biblical scholar of the early church. He pioneered systematic, verse-by-verse commentary, compiled the Hexapla (a six-column comparison of Old Testament texts), and developed the allegorical method of interpretation that shaped later exegesis. Though some of his speculative teachings were condemned at the Second Council of Constantinople (553), his learning and his commentaries — including those on Matthew and John — remained deeply influential on Fathers East and West.
